data.name: CF-API-Exfil — Bulk Data Exfiltration via api.soledrop.co
data.description: Detects bulk data exfiltration and sensitive-endpoint enumeration through the SoleDrop internal API gateway (class_uid 4002, 'HTTP Requests'). Scopes to the API surface (url_string contains '/api/v1/') and flags requests hitting exfil-sensitive routes ('/export', '/download', 'training-data', 'include_weights', '/customers', '/billing', '/models'). Groups by src_ip and fires on either signal: (a) sensitive_hits >= 10 — a single source pulling from data-bearing endpoints at volume — OR (b) max_bytes >= 1048576, a single response body over 1 MiB (EdgeResponseBytes is a compressed string, cast with number()). Emits the largest response's URI (max_by on bytes) and distinct_paths as enumeration context. Low false-positive because normal API clients neither iterate 10+ sensitive endpoints nor pull megabyte payloads from /export in a 5-minute window; both floors are enforced in-query (rule threshold kept at >0). Byte floor is tunable per traffic profile. MITRE ATT&CK T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1119 (Automated Collection), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Covers OneFlare scenario 06.

scheduledParams.query:
class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests'
| let src_ip       = src_endpoint.ip
| let uri          = http_request.url.url_string
| let bytes        = number(unmapped.EdgeResponseBytes)
| let is_sensitive = uri contains:anycase("/export") OR uri contains:anycase("/download") OR uri contains:anycase("training-data") OR uri contains:anycase("include_weights") OR uri contains:anycase("/customers") OR uri contains:anycase("/billing") OR uri contains:anycase("/models")
| filter uri contains:anycase("/api/v1/")
| group
    api_requests   = count(),
    sensitive_hits = count(is_sensitive),
    distinct_paths = estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path),
    max_bytes      = max(bytes),
    largest_uri    = max_by(uri, bytes),
    host           = any(http_request.url.hostname),
    country        = any(src_endpoint.location.country),
    first_seen     = oldest(timestamp),
    last_seen      = newest(timestamp)
  by src_ip
| filter sensitive_hits >= 10 OR max_bytes >= 1048576
| let detection_time = simpledateformat(last_seen, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z', 'America/Chicago')
| sort -sensitive_hits
| columns detection_time, src_ip, host, api_requests, sensitive_hits, distinct_paths, max_bytes, largest_uri, country, first_seen
| limit 100
